theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: Today we're asking: should we pay more for chocolate?
sentences.korean: 오늘 질문은: 초콜릿에 더 많이 지불해야 할까요?

sentences.english: I do love a bit of dark chocolate.
sentences.korean: 나는 다크 초콜릿을 정말 좋아해.

sentences.english: Do you know, I really like the one that's got some chilli in it.
sentences.korean: 있잖아, 칠리가 들어간 걸 정말 좋아해.

sentences.english: What's your favourite, Becca?
sentences.korean: 네가 가장 좋아하는 건 뭐야, 베카?

sentences.english: Oh, I also love dark chocolate, but chilli is a bit spicy for me.
sentences.korean: 오, 나도 다크 초콜릿을 좋아하는데, 칠리는 나한테 좀 매워.

quiz.question: What does '사회적 기업 — 지역사회를 돕기 위한 사업체' mean in English?
options: to trace something back to something

theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: I like it with sea salt.
sentences.korean: 바다 소금이 든 게 좋아.

sentences.english: But now to the stuff that makes chocolate possible — cocoa — a key ingredient and it's become more expensive in recent years.
sentences.korean: 이제 초콜릿을 만드는 재료 — 코코아 — 에 대해 이야기해보죠. 핵심 재료인데 최근 몇 년간 더 비싸졌어요.

sentences.english: Now, a new EU law could make chocolate even more expensive.
sentences.korean: 새로운 EU 법이 초콜릿을 더욱 비싸게 만들 수 있어요.

sentences.english: Today, we'll talk about how this law could change things for farmers, companies and chocolate lovers.
sentences.korean: 오늘은 이 법이 농부, 기업, 초콜릿 애호가들에게 어떤 변화를 가져올 수 있는지 이야기할 거예요.

sentences.english: Europeans are big fans of chocolate, just like us!
sentences.korean: 유럽인들은 우리처럼 초콜릿의 열렬한 팬이에요!

quiz.question: What does '위치 정보 — 사람이나 물건의 정확한 위치를 보여주는 데이터' mean in English?
options: to trace something back to something

theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: Around how many tonnes of cocoa beans were imported to Europe in 2023?
sentences.korean: 2023년에 약 몇 톤의 코코아 콩이 유럽에 수입되었을까요?

sentences.english: Well, I have no idea, but I'm just going to guess and say 500,000 tonnes.
sentences.korean: 글쎄, 전혀 모르겠는데, 그냥 추측해서 50만 톤이라고 할게.

sentences.english: Now, that new EU law would stop companies buying cocoa from land where any trees were cut down in the last six years.
sentences.korean: 새 EU 법은 지난 6년간 나무가 베어진 토지에서 코코아를 사는 것을 기업들에게 금지할 거예요.

sentences.english: The process of cutting down trees is called deforestation.
sentences.korean: 나무를 베는 과정을 산림 벌채라고 해요.

sentences.english: Albert Smith, who owns a small chocolate business, believes that the new law wouldn't change too much for his company.
sentences.korean: 소규모 초콜릿 사업을 운영하는 앨버트 스미스는 새 법이 자기 회사에 크게 영향을 주지 않을 거라고 믿어요.

quiz.question: What does '외딴 — 사람들이 사는 곳에서 먼' mean in English?

theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: We have all the information on farm level already because the producers that we work with are small plantations that either work through a co-op or social enterprise and all of our cocoa is already traced back to the plantation.
sentences.korean: 우리는 농장 수준의 모든 정보를 이미 가지고 있어요. 함께 일하는 생산자들이 협동조합이나 사회적 기업을 통해 운영하는 소규모 농장이고, 우리의 모든 코코아는 이미 농장까지 추적 가능하거든요.

sentences.english: Albert adds that they already know where the cocoa beans come from, as they work with farmers from co-ops or social enterprises.
sentences.korean: 앨버트는 협동조합이나 사회적 기업 농부들과 함께 일하기 때문에 코코아 콩이 어디서 오는지 이미 안다고 덧붙여요.

sentences.english: Mapping means that farmers have to actually provide the geolocations of their plantation.
sentences.korean: 매핑이란 농부들이 농장의 위치 정보를 실제로 제공해야 한다는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: And you know, for some farmers that are very remote, they might not have access to the internet or the technology.
sentences.korean: 매우 외딴 곳에 있는 일부 농부들은 인터넷이나 기술에 접근할 수 없을 수도 있거든요.

sentences.english: Absolutely I would do that because I think the normal chocolates are way too cheap.
sentences.korean: 당연히 그렇게 할 거예요.

quiz.question: What does '협동조합 — 노동자가 소유하는 농장/사업체' mean in English?
options: to trace something back to something

theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: Definitely we will pay, we have to pay more because the money will go to the local people, to give them a better life, also to tackle deforestation.
sentences.korean: 일반 초콜릿이 너무 싸다고 생각하거든요.

sentences.english: For me, personally, absolutely not!
sentences.korean: 저한테는 개인적으로 절대 아니에요!

sentences.english: Out of principle, because I want them to be produced most efficiently as possible.
sentences.korean: 원칙적으로 가능한 한 효율적으로 생산되기를 원해요.

sentences.english: The cost is completely irrelevant.
sentences.korean: 비용은 완전히 관계없어요.

sentences.english: Mixed opinions, it seems!
sentences.korean: 의견이 엇갈리네요!

quiz.question: What does '~의 출처를 추적하다' mean in English?
options: to trace something back to something

theme: Should We Pay More for Chocolate?

sentences.english: Whilst one chocolate lover would pay more to support farmers, the other says the production process should be as efficient as possible.
sentences.korean: 한 초콜릿 애호가는 농부를 지원하기 위해 더 내겠다고 하고, 다른 사람은 생산 과정이 가능한 한 효율적이어야 한다고 해요.

sentences.english: OK, time to answer our quiz question.
sentences.korean: 퀴즈 답을 말할 시간이에요.

sentences.english: I asked you how many tonnes of cocoa were imported to Europe in 2023 and you said 500,000 tonnes.
sentences.korean: 2023년에 유럽에 수입된 코코아가 얼마나 되냐고 물었고 50만 톤이라고 했죠.

sentences.english: Well, Neil, it is 2 million tonnes!
sentences.korean: 닐, 200만 톤이에요!

quiz.question: What does '산림 벌채 — 나무를 베는 과정' mean in English?
options: to trace something back to something
